ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and finely chopped
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) dried wood ear mushrooms, soaked and finely chopped

ingredients.item: 7 ounces tofu (200 g), finely crumbled with a fork
ingredients.original: 7 ounces tofu (200 g), finely crumbled with a fork

ingredients.item: 1 ounce (28 g) leek (white part only), finely chopped
ingredients.original: 1 ounce (28 g) leek (white part only), finely chopped

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon sugar, plus 1 tablespoon for soaking rice paper wrappers
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon sugar, plus 1 tablespoon for soaking rice paper wrappers

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon vegetable or mushroom stock powder

ingredients.item: 20 rice paper or spring roll wrappers (6 inches/15 cm)
ingredients.original: 20 rice paper or spring roll wrappers (6 inches/15 cm)

ingredients.item: 1 tablespoon lime juice, for soaking rice paper
ingredients.original: 1 tablespoon lime juice, for soaking rice paper

ingredients.item: 2 cups (480 ml) water, to soften rice paper
ingredients.original: 2 cups (480 ml) water, to soften rice paper

ingredients.item: ½ cup (120 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce, for serving
ingredients.original: ½ cup (120 ml) vegan fish dipping sauce, for serving

ingredients.item: 1. Using a knife or mandoline, shave corn kernels off cobs into a bowl and set aside.
ingredients.original: 1. Using a knife or mandoline, shave corn kernels off cobs into a bowl and set aside.

ingredients.item: 2. In a large bowl, combine mushrooms, cilantro, tofu, leek, 1 teaspoon of sugar, salt, stock powder, and pepper. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2. In a large bowl, combine mushrooms, cilantro, tofu, leek, 1 teaspoon of sugar, salt, stock powder, and pepper. Mix well and set aside for 15 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3. Prepare rice paper wrappers for rolling with the lime juice, water, and 1 tablespoon of sugar (here).
ingredients.original: 3. Prepare rice paper wrappers for rolling with the lime juice, water, and 1 tablespoon of sugar (here).

ingredients.item: 4. To assemble the rolls, scoop 1 tablespoon of filling on one end of a wrapper, and then spread until it covers about 4 inches (10 cm), leaving some room on the sides. Lift that end of the wrapper and fold it over the filling. Push the covered filling back a bit, and then fold in both sides. Continue to roll tightly to the other end. Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
ingredients.original: 4. To assemble the rolls, scoop 1 tablespoon of filling on one end of a wrapper, and then spread until it covers about 4 inches (10 cm), leaving some room on the sides. Lift that end of the wrapper and fold it over the filling. Push the covered filling back a bit, and then fold in both sides. Continue to roll tightly to the other end. Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.

ingredients.item: 5. Cut a piece of parchment paper to line the bottom of a medium saucepan or skillet. Fill the bottom of the saucepan with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and heat to 360°F (180°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying.)
ingredients.original: 5. Cut a piece of parchment paper to line the bottom of a medium saucepan or skillet. Fill the bottom of the saucepan with 2 inches (5 cm) of oil and heat to 360°F (180°C). (You can check the temperature with a cooking thermometer or insert one end of a chopstick in the oil; if bubbles appear around it, the oil is ready for deep-frying.)

ingredients.item: 6. Drop rolls in the pan, seam sides down so that they remain sealed when they touch the hot oil. Deep-fry in batches until golden brown (5 to 8 minutes). When done, use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer rolls to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.
ingredients.original: 6. Drop rolls in the pan, seam sides down so that they remain sealed when they touch the hot oil. Deep-fry in batches until golden brown (5 to 8 minutes). When done, use a wire spider or slotted spoon to transfer rolls to a cooling rack or paper towel–lined plate to drain.

ingredients.item: 7. Serve warm with vegan fish dipping sauce.
ingredients.original: 7. Serve warm with vegan fish dipping sauce.
